siteConfig.podcastName: Houston Abogado de Accidentes w. Ben Dominguez

episode.title: Your Rights After a Houston Car Accident, with Ben Dominguez
episode.description: Join solo-practice Houston attorney Ben Dominguez as he walks accident victims across Houston and Harris County through Texas personal injury law, the two-year statute of limitations, modified comparative fault, dealing with insurance adjusters, and the decisions a driver faces in the days after a serious collision. Bilingual conversations in English and Spanish are grounded in 30+ years of Texas practice.

attorney.name: Ben Dominguez
attorney.title: Personal Injury & Products Liability Attorney, Podcast Host
bio: Ben Dominguez II is a solo-practice Houston personal injury and products liability attorney admitted to the State Bar of Texas in 1993 (Bar No. 00786415). A fourth-generation Texan, he established his Houston practice in 1994 after earning his J.D. from Vermont Law School in 1990 and a Master of Studies in Environmental Law (MSEL) from Vermont Law School in 1995. Ben Dominguez is bilingual and handles cases in English and Spanish for clients throughout the Greater Houston area and Harris County.

Ben Dominguez's practice initially covered personal injury, securities litigation, criminal defense, and business litigation. In 2015 he narrowed the practice to personal injury and products liability, car accidents, truck accidents, motorcycle accidents, bicycle and pedestrian cases, rideshare (Uber) collisions, construction injuries, premises liability, and wrongful death. He is admitted to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit and the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Texas.

Beyond case work, Ben Dominguez hosts Houston Abogado de Accidentes, a bilingual podcast focused on Texas personal injury law, insurance coverage, and what accident victims across Houston actually need to know before talking to an adjuster. He is a member of the Texas Trial Lawyers Association (TTLA), the American Association for Justice (AAJ), and the Pan-European Organisation of Personal Injury Lawyers (PEOPIL).

contact.address: The Museum Tower, 4899 Montrose Blvd., Suite 1306, Houston, TX 77006

faqGroups.label: About the Podcast
faqGroups.description: Learn more about Houston Abogado de Accidentes and what you can expect from each episode.

questions.question: What topics does Houston Abogado de Accidentes cover?
questions.answer: The podcast covers personal injury topics relevant to Houston and Harris County drivers, car, truck, motorcycle, bicycle, pedestrian, rideshare, and construction accidents, plus products liability, premises liability, and wrongful death. Ben Dominguez focuses on what Texas law actually allows and requires, including the two-year statute of limitations, Texas modified comparative fault, and how insurance adjusters evaluate claims in Texas.

questions.question: Who hosts the podcast?
questions.answer: Ben Dominguez II hosts Houston Abogado de Accidentes. He has been a Texas-licensed attorney since 1993 (Bar No. 00786415) and runs a solo practice at 4899 Montrose Blvd., Suite 1306, Houston, TX 77006.

questions.question: Is the podcast available in Spanish?
questions.answer: Yes. Ben Dominguez is bilingual and serves Spanish-speaking clients across the Greater Houston area. Episodes include Spanish-language discussion where useful, and consultations with the firm are available in English or Spanish.

questions.question: Can I submit questions for the podcast?
questions.answer: Yes. Contact the firm through benwins.com or call (713) 804-6822. Listener questions from Houston and Harris County are welcome, particularly from Spanish-speaking community members.

faqGroups.label: Legal Questions
faqGroups.description: Common Texas personal injury law questions answered by solo-practice Houston attorney Ben Dominguez.

questions.question: What should I do immediately after a car accident in Houston?
questions.answer: Seek medical attention, even if injuries seem minor, because Texas courts weigh the gap between the accident and the first medical record. Document the scene with photos, exchange information with other drivers, and request a Texas Peace Officer's Crash Report (CR-3) from the responding agency. Do not give a recorded statement to any insurance adjuster, yours or the other driver's, before speaking with a Texas personal injury attorney. Evidence like surveillance video on Houston commercial corridors is often overwritten within 7 to 30 days.

questions.question: How long do I have to file a personal injury claim in Texas?
questions.answer: Texas Civil Practice & Remedies Code Section 16.003 sets a two-year statute of limitations for most personal injury claims, running from the date of the accident or injury. Claims against government entities (including the City of Houston and Harris County) carry shorter notice requirements under the Texas Tort Claims Act. Missing the deadline typically forfeits the claim entirely.

questions.question: How does Texas comparative fault affect my case?
questions.answer: Texas follows modified comparative fault under Civil Practice & Remedies Code Section 33.001. If you are 50% or less at fault, you may still recover damages, reduced by your percentage of fault. If you are 51% or more at fault, you recover nothing. Insurance adjusters routinely try to push the injured driver's fault percentage above that threshold.

questions.question: What kinds of cases does Ben Dominguez handle?
questions.answer: Ben Dominguez's practice covers car accidents, truck accidents, motorcycle accidents, bicycle and pedestrian accidents, Uber and rideshare collisions, construction accidents, premises liability, products liability, and wrongful death cases throughout the Greater Houston area.

questions.question: Do I need a lawyer if the insurance company offers me a settlement?
questions.answer: Consult with a Texas-licensed personal injury attorney before accepting any offer. Early offers in Texas auto cases often fail to account for future medical care, lost earning capacity, or the full scope of impairment. Ben Dominguez Law offers free consultations in English and Spanish.

content.heroTitle: Houston Abogado de Accidentes w. Ben Dominguez
content.heroDescription: Join solo-practice Houston attorney Ben Dominguez as he walks accident victims across Houston and Harris County through Texas personal injury law, the two-year statute of limitations, modified comparative fault, dealing with insurance adjusters, and the decisions a driver faces in the days after a serious collision. Bilingual conversations (English / Spanish) grounded in 30+ years of Texas practice.
content.articleTitle: Bilingual Houston Personal Injury Attorney Serving the Greater Houston Area Since 1994
articleParagraphs: Houston Abogado de Accidentes brings the judgment Ben Dominguez has built over 30 years of Texas personal injury and products liability practice directly to accident victims in Houston and Harris County. Ben Dominguez has been a Texas-licensed attorney since 1993 (Bar No. 00786415), runs a solo practice at 4899 Montrose Blvd., and handles cases in English and Spanish for clients throughout the Greater Houston area.

Each episode addresses what Houston drivers, riders, and pedestrians actually raise on intake calls: the two-year statute of limitations under Texas Civil Practice & Remedies Code Section 16.003, Texas modified comparative fault under Section 33.001, how to respond when an adjuster calls the day after the crash, and why evidence preservation on Houston freeways and surface streets often matters more than the initial medical record. Ben Dominguez's podcast covers the practical answers he gives clients in the office.
content.featuredQuote: The two most important things a Houston accident victim can do in the first 48 hours are simple, see a doctor, and stop talking to the other driver's insurance company until you have counsel.

footer.description: Houston Abogado de Accidentes is a bilingual (English / Spanish) podcast hosted by Ben Dominguez, a solo-practice Houston personal injury and products liability attorney serving the Greater Houston area since 1994. Ben Dominguez is admitted to the State Bar of Texas (1993) and handles personal injury, products liability, and wrongful death matters throughout Harris County.

topicalEntryGrid.title: Explore Ben Dominguez's Practice Areas
topicalEntryGrid.description: Browse the Texas accident topics Ben Dominguez covers on the podcast. Each card links to a deeper resource from Ben Dominguez Law on that situation.

links.title: Car Accident Lawyer
links.description: Ben Dominguez represents Houston drivers and passengers injured in serious collisions, from I-10 and 610 Loop crashes to surface-street impacts across Harris County.

links.title: Truck Accident Lawyer
links.description: Commercial truck and 18-wheeler cases in Texas involve federal motor carrier regulations, multiple liable parties, and catastrophic injuries.

links.title: Motorcycle Accident Lawyer
links.description: Motorcycle cases in Texas involve insurer bias and modified comparative fault. Ben Dominguez pushes back on assumptions about rider conduct.

links.title: Uber / Rideshare Accident Lawyer
links.description: Uber and Lyft collisions in Texas layer the Texas Transportation Code over standard car-accident liability, with multiple insurance policies often in play.

links.title: Bicycle Accident Lawyer
links.description: Houston cyclist cases often involve disputed fault. Ben Dominguez investigates intersection evidence and rebuts comparative-fault arguments.

links.title: Pedestrian Accident Lawyer
links.description: Pedestrian strikes in Houston often involve distracted driving and disputed right-of-way. Evidence preservation matters within days.

links.title: Wrongful Death
links.description: Texas Civil Practice & Remedies Code Section 71.004 governs wrongful death claims. Ben Dominguez represents surviving spouses, children, and parents.

links.title: Construction Accident Lawyer
links.description: Construction injuries across the Houston area often involve third-party contractors, OSHA issues, and layered insurance coverage.

links.title: Traumatic Brain Injury
links.description: TBI cases require life-care planning for future treatment, assistive equipment, and lost earning capacity. Ben Dominguez documents long-term damages.

links.title: Spinal Cord Injury & Paralysis
links.description: Catastrophic spinal injuries involve lifelong care costs. Ben Dominguez works with medical experts to build the full damages picture.

links.title: Slip-and-Fall & Premises Liability
links.description: Slip-and-fall and premises cases across Houston properties under Texas premises liability doctrine (invitee / licensee / trespasser distinctions).

links.title: Free Bilingual Consultation
links.description: Consultations in English or Spanish. No fee unless you win. Call (713) 804-6822 or reach the firm through benwins.com.

links.title: About Ben Dominguez
links.description: Solo-practice Houston personal injury and products liability attorney. Texas Bar since 1993, bilingual representation across the Greater Houston area.

links.title: Houston Catastrophic Injury Lawyer
links.description: Catastrophic injury cases in Houston, where life-care planning and long-term damages models drive value.

links.title: Personal Injury Practice Areas
links.description: Auto, truck, motorcycle, pedestrian, premises, and catastrophic injury matters across Houston and Harris County.

links.title: Contact Ben Dominguez
links.description: Free case review, contingency fee, no recovery means no fee. Bilingual intake.

disclaimers.text: This is an advertisement for legal services under the Texas Disciplinary Rules of Professional Conduct 7.01-7.05. The information on this website and podcast is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice.

Viewing this site, listening to the podcast, or communicating with Ben Dominguez Law does not create an attorney-client relationship. No attorney-client relationship exists unless and until a written fee agreement has been signed by both the client and a Ben Dominguez Law attorney.
Past results do not guarantee future outcomes. Each case is unique and must be evaluated on its own merits. The outcome of any prior case does not predict a similar result in your case.
Any client testimonials, endorsements, or reviews shown on this site reflect the individual experiences of those clients, are not a guarantee of similar results, and were not compensated.
Ben Dominguez is licensed to practice in the State of Texas. This website is not intended as a solicitation for legal services in any jurisdiction where Ben Dominguez is not licensed to practice.

Contingency Fee Notice: No attorney's fees are charged unless a recovery is obtained on your behalf. Clients may be responsible for case-related costs (expert witness fees, court costs, medical records) as outlined in a written fee agreement. Fee structure is negotiable.

Content shared on Houston Abogado de Accidentes is for general informational and educational purposes only, is not legal advice, and should not be relied upon as a substitute for consultation with a licensed Texas attorney about the specific facts of your matter.

The attorney responsible for the content of this podcast and website under Texas Disciplinary Rule 7.04 is Ben Dominguez II (Bar No. 00786415), Ben Dominguez Law Firm, 4899 Montrose Blvd., Suite 1306, Houston, TX 77006.
